segments.paradiso-5-g85: Avete is the second-person plural present indicative of avere (“you have”). Il novo e ’l vecchio Testamento uses the definite articles (il, contracted ’l) with novo (archaic spelling of nuovo = “new”) and vecchio (“old”) to name the two Testaments; word order is normal (verb — direct objects). Testamento is a noun with the article and completes what you have.
segments.paradiso-5-g86: e ’l pastor de la Chiesa che vi guida links another item with e (“and”). ’l (lo) is the article before pastor, here meaning the spiritual shepherd (the Church’s leader); de = of, la Chiesa = the Church (institution), and che introduces a relative clause (who) with vi as the clitic object meaning “you” and guida as present indicative of guidare (“guides”).
segments.paradiso-5-g87: questo is a demonstrative pronoun (“this”). vi is the clitic meaning “to you” or “for you.” basti is the third-person singular present subjunctive of bastare used jussively/optatively (’may/let this suffice’). a introduces the goal/benefit (a vostro salvamento = “for your salvation”), with vostro agreeing as your and salvamento = salvation.
segments.paradiso-5-g88: Se is the conditional/subordinating conjunction “if.” mala is an adjective modifying cupidigia and here means “evil/bad.” cupidigia is a feminine noun meaning “greed/desire.” altro functions as an adverb/pronoun “something else/elsewhere.” vi is the clitic meaning “to you,” and grida is third-person singular present indicative of gridare (“cries out”); together the clause means “if bad greed calls something else to you.”
segments.paradiso-5-g89: uomini is the vocative/accusative plural meaning “men” or “human beings” used here as an exhortation. siate is the second-person plural present subjunctive of essere used as a polite/poetic imperative: “be (you) men.”
segments.paradiso-5-g90: e non pecore matte is a negative admonition: e (“and”) + non (“not”) + pecore (plural noun “sheep”) + matte (adjective “mad/frantic/folly-struck”) — literally “and not frantic sheep,” contrasting with the previous exhortation to be men.
segments.paradiso-5-g91: sì che introduces a result clause meaning “so that.” ’l Giudeo is the subject “the Jew.” di voi ('of you') with tra voi (‘among you’) specifies location/position; non rida is negative plus the present subjunctive of ridere (here used jussively/optatively: “may he not laugh [at you]” or “so that he not mock you”).
segments.paradiso-5-g92: Non fate is the negative command (non + second-person plural imperative/indicative of fare) meaning “do not act.” com’ is a poetic contraction of come = “like/as.” agnel (archaic agnello) is “lamb.” che introduces a relative clause; lascia is third-person singular present indicative of lasciare (“leaves”), il latte = “the milk,” de la sua madre = “of its mother” (de = of/from; sua agrees with agnel).
segments.paradiso-5-g93: e semplice e lascivo lists two adjectives describing the lamb: semplice (“simple/naïve”) and lascivo (“lascivious/wanton”), joined by e. seco medesmo is a reflexive prepositional phrase (‘with itself’/‘to itself’), a in a suo piacer indicates purpose/measure “to/for its own pleasure,” and combatte is third-person singular present indicative of combattere meaning “it fights/struggles” (here reflexively, “it wrestles with itself”).
segments.paradiso-5-g94: This segment is a single punctuation mark (period) marking the end of the quoted admonition; no lexical items to analyze.
segments.paradiso-5-g29: Or is an adverb here meaning 'now' at the start of the clause; ti is the dative pronoun 'to you'; parrà is a future form meaning 'will seem' (from parere) — together Ti parrà = 'it will seem to you'. The verb is in the future, giving a judgment the listener is expected to have.
segments.paradiso-5-g30: Se introduces a conditional 'if'. Tu is the subject pronoun 'you'. Quinci is an adverb 'from here' / 'on this basis'. Argomenti is the 2nd-person form meaning 'you argue / reason' (present), so the whole phrase = 'if you argue from here'.
segments.paradiso-5-g31: L' is the definite article 'the' contracted before a vowel; alto is an adjective 'lofty' modifying valor; valor is a noun 'value/worth'; del is the contraction di + il 'of the'; voto is the noun 'vow' (a solemn promise). The word order is normal noun phrase: 'the lofty value of the vow.'
segments.paradiso-5-g32: S' è fatto is the reflexive/impersonal construction si è fatto 'has been made' or 'is so made' (s' = si). Sì is an adverb 'so' / 'thus'. Che introduces the subordinate clause 'that...'. Dio consenta is present subjunctive ('consenta') used inside a clause expressing the content/result of 'si è fatto', and quando tu consenti is a temporal clause 'when you consent' (consenti = present indicative). Overall: 'if it is so made that God consents when you consent.'
segments.paradiso-5-g33: Ché is a causal conjunction meaning 'for' or 'because', introducing the explanation that follows.
segments.paradiso-5-g34: Nel = in + il, 'in the/while in'. Fermar is the infinitive used as a verbal noun here: 'in establishing/fixing' (fermar = fermare, 'to fix/establish'). Tra = 'between'; Dio e l'omo = 'God and man'; il patto = 'the pact/agreement'. Word order places the verb-noun phrase first: 'in establishing the pact between God and man.'
segments.paradiso-5-g35: Vittima is a noun here meaning 'a victim' or better 'a sacrifice' in this figurative/legal sense. Fassi is an archaic/poetic form of farsi (si fa) meaning 'is made / becomes' (reflexive/passive sense). Di questo tesoro = 'of this treasure' (meaning the thing in question becomes the sacrifice).
segments.paradiso-5-g36: Tal quale is a fixed expression meaning 'such as' / 'of the kind which'; io dico is a parenthetical 'I say' clarifying the referent. The phrase qualifies the 'treasure' just mentioned.
segments.paradiso-5-g37: E = 'and'. Fassi again is farsi 'is made / becomes' (passive/reflexive). Col is the contraction con + il 'with the' or 'by the'; suo atto = 'his act' (atto = 'act'). So: 'and is made so by his act.'
segments.paradiso-5-g38: Dunque is a discourse adverb 'so/therefore'. Che here is an interrogative 'what'. Render is the infinitive 'to return / to give back' (render = rendere). Puossi is an archaic/impersonal form from potere (puòsi / puossi) expressing 'can one' or 'is it possible' — it makes the question impersonal. Per ristoro = 'as/for restitution / as recompense' (per = 'for', ristoro = 'recompense/restore' in this sense).
segments.paradiso-5-g39: Se = 'if'. Credi is 2nd person singular present 'you believe / think'. Bene is an adverb 'well / properly'. Usar is the infinitive 'to use'. Quel = demonstrative 'that' (quel che = 'that which'). C' here stands for che (relative 'which/that') introducing the subordinate clause; hai offerto = 'you have offered'. The noun phrase 'quel ch'hai offerto' = 'that which you have offered (me/ us)'.
segments.paradiso-5-g40: Di here is a preposition 'of/from'. Maltolletto is a noun meaning 'an ill-natured person / rascal' (a pejorative label). Vuo' is an archaic form of vuoi (2nd person singular of volere) 'you want / intend'. Far = infinitive 'to make / to do'. Buon lavoro = 'good work' or 'a good result'. The whole clause says the subject intends to make good use / get good result out of (or from) a maltolletto.
segments.paradiso-5-g41: Tu = 'you'. Se' (sei) is the 2nd person singular of essere 'are' (here written se'), so 'you are'. Omai = 'by now / already'. Del = di + il 'of the'. Maggior punto = 'main / greater point' (maggior modifies punto). Certo = 'certain'. The phrase = 'You are now certain of the main point.'
segments.paradiso-5-g42: Ma = 'but'. Perché here = 'because / since' introducing a reason. Santa Chiesa = 'the Holy Church' (Santa modifying Chiesa). In ciò = 'in this' / 'in this matter'. Dispensa is a present-tense verb 'grants a dispensation / exempts' (Santa Chiesa ... dispensa = 'the Church dispenses / grants dispensations' in this context).
segments.paradiso-5-g43: Che = 'which / that' (introducing a relative clause). Par = present 'seems / appears'. Contra = 'against / contrary to'. Lo ver = 'the truth' (ver = verità). Ch' i' t'ho scoverto = 'that I have uncovered / revealed to you' (ch' = che, i' = io, t' = to you, ho scoverto = I have revealed). The clause means 'which seems contrary to the truth I have revealed to you.'
segments.paradiso-5-g44: Convienti is an archaic/poetic form related to conviene: an impersonal construction meaning 'it is fitting / proper'. Ancor = 'still / yet'. Sedere is the infinitive 'to sit' or 'to remain seated'. Un poco = 'a little'. A mensa = 'at table' / 'at the meal' (mensa here means meal/table). So: 'it is still fitting to sit a little at table.'
segments.paradiso-5-g45: Però che = 'because / inasmuch as'. 'L cibo = the food/meal (’l = il). Rigido = 'rigid / harsh / hard' describing the food (i.e. solid or severe diet). C' hai preso = contraction of che + hai preso 'which you have taken / eaten'. The clause explains the reason: 'because the harsh food you have taken...'
segments.paradiso-5-g46: Richiede is 3rd person singular present 'requires / needs'. Ancora = 'still'. Aiuto = 'help / aid'. A tua dispensa = 'from your dispensa' — dispensa here is a noun meaning 'provisions / pantry / supply' (i.e. your earthly provision). So: 'still requires aid from your provisions.'
segments.paradiso-5-g47: Apri is the 2nd-person singular imperative of aprire ('open'), addressed to 'you'. 'la mente' is the direct object ('the mind'). 'a quel ch’io ti paleso' is a prepositional phrase: a + quel (that) + ch’ (che, 'which/that') introducing the relative clause 'io ti paleso' ('I reveal to you'); 'ti' is the indirect object clitic. 'fermalvi' is an archaic imperative form meaning 'fix/secure it' composed of the verb (ferma-) plus enclitic pronouns: -l- (it) and -vi (there/in it), so 'fix it there'. 'entro' is an adverb meaning 'within/inside', reinforcing the location.
segments.paradiso-5-g48: Ché here = 'because/for'. Non is the sentential negation. Fa is 3rd-person singular present of fare used idiomatically with scïenza to mean 'counts as / constitutes' (literally 'makes knowledge'). Scïenza means 'knowledge' or 'understanding' and is the complement of fa.
segments.paradiso-5-g49: Sanza is an archaic form of senza = 'without'. Lo is the masculine singular direct object article/pronoun ('it/the'). Ritenere here means 'to hold/keep in mind' (retain); the infinitive follows the preposition sanza to indicate what is lacking.
segments.paradiso-5-g50: Avere inteso is a perfect infinitive construction meaning 'to have understood' (avere as auxiliary + past participle inteso). The whole phrase functions as the nominal content that, without being kept in mind, does not amount to knowledge.
segments.paradiso-5-g51: 'Due cose' is the subject ('two things'). 'Si convegnono' is a middle/impersonal use of convegnere (3rd-person plural present) meaning 'are fitting' or 'becoming appropriate'; the clitic si marks the reflexive/middle voice. 'a l’essenza' = 'to the essence' (l’ = the). 'di questo sacrificio' is a genitive phrase 'of this sacrifice' qualifying essenza.
segments.paradiso-5-g52: L’una = 'the one' (feminine). È is 3rd-person singular present of essere. 'Quella di che si fa' is an archaic relative construction: 'quella di che' = 'that which' and 'si fa' is the impersonal/patient form of fare meaning 'is done' or 'is performed' (literally 'one does').
segments.paradiso-5-g53: L’altr’ (elided l' + altra) = 'the other'. È is 'is'. 'La convenenza' is the definite noun meaning 'the propriety' or 'the fitting form/ceremonial propriety'—the second thing fitting to the essence described.
segments.paradiso-5-g54: Quest’ultima = 'this latter' (the convenience/propriety just mentioned). Già mai non ... is a negative intensifier (in medieval usage yielding 'never'). Si cancella is a reflexive/middle present meaning 'is erased' or 'is annulled'. 'Se non servata' = 'unless preserved/observed' (servata = past participle 'kept/observed'). Note: the 'se' here is the conjunction 'if/unless' (the supplied senses list did not include that sense).
segments.paradiso-5-g55: E intorno di lei = 'and concerning it' (di lei = 'of/around it'). Sì preciso di sopra si favella is a passive/impersonal statement: 'it is spoken (favella) above so precisely' — di sopra = 'above' (earlier in the text), si favella = 'one speaks' or 'is spoken'.
segments.paradiso-5-g56: Però = 'therefore'. Necessitato fu is a passive/periphrastic past: necessitato (past participle 'compelled') + fu (was) = 'was compelled'. 'A li Ebrei' = 'to the Jews' (dative of people). Pur = 'even/still' as an intensifier. L’offerere is the infinitive offerere with an attached clitic l' = 'to offer it' (the thing referred to).
segments.paradiso-5-g57: Ancor ch’ = 'even though/although' (concessive). Alcuna offerta = 'some offering' or 'some particular offering'. Sì permutasse: permutasse is subjunctive (concessive mood) of permutare meaning 'might be changed/exchanged', and sì = 'thus/so'. The whole clause concedes that some offerings were altered.
segments.paradiso-5-g58: Come = 'as' or 'in the way that'. Saver is the archaic infinitive for sapere ('to know'); Dei is the contraction de + i = 'of the' or 'of them' depending on reference. The short phrase is elliptical/archaic; literally 'as to know of them' (i.e. 'as could be known about them' or 'as was known of them').
segments.paradiso-5-g59: L’altra = 'the other' (elided article + altra). This introduces the second of the two things previously mentioned.
segments.paradiso-5-g60: Che is the relative pronoun 'which'. 'Per materia' = 'by/through matter' (here 'by its subject-matter' or 'in respect of the matter'). 'T’è' = clitic dative ti + è (is) = 'is to you' or 'has been to you'. Aperta is the past participle/adjective 'open/plain', agreeing with altra: 'opened/laid bare to you'.
segments.paradiso-5-g61: Puote is an archaic spelling of può (3rd-person singular present), here meaning 'can'. Ben is an adverb intensifier 'well' or 'indeed'. Esser is the infinitive 'to be'. Tal = 'such' (a quality/condition). Together: 'it can well be such'.
segments.paradiso-5-g62: Che introduces a result/relative clause: 'that'. 'Non si falla' uses the reflexive/middle si + verb fallare (here meaning 'go wrong / be at fault / be invalidated') in the present; 'non si falla' = 'it does not fail / is not invalidated'. 'Se' is the conditional conjunction 'if' (the provided sense list did not include this sense). 'Con altra materia' = 'with another matter' (con = with). 'Si converta' is present subjunctive/passive-like middle (convertare) meaning 'is/should be converted/changed'; the si is again the reflexive/middle marker. The whole clause says the thing remains valid even if the matter is changed to another.
segments.paradiso-5-g1: S’io is an archaic contraction of se io (= 'if I'). Ti is the direct object 'you'. Fiammeggio is 1st‑person singular present of the poetic verb fiammeggiare ('I blaze / set aflame'). Nel caldo d’amore is a prepositional phrase ('in the warmth of love'), and di là dal modo introduces a spatial/comparative sense 'from beyond the manner' with là + dal modo; the relative clause che ’n terra si vede uses the impersonal/passive si vede ('is seen' / 'one sees it') referring back to modo.
segments.paradiso-5-g2: Sì che introduces a result clause ('so that'). Del viso tuo is a genitive phrase 'of your face' (poetic placement before the verb). Vinco is 1st‑person present of vincere ('I overcome / I conquer'), and il valore is the object meaning 'the power/force' (of the countenance). Word order is slightly inverted for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-5-g3: Non + imperative: non ti maravigliar is a negative imperative addressed to 'you' (ti), where maravigliar is an archaic/poetic form of maravigliare ('be astonished' / 'marvel'). The verb is used in the sense 'do not be amazed.'
segments.paradiso-5-g4: Ché = perché ('for / because'). Ciò is a demonstrative pronoun 'this/that'. Procede is 3rd‑singular present of procedere ('proceeds / comes from'). Da perfetto veder is a prepositional phrase: da + noun phrase in which perfetto qualifies veder (the infinitive used nominally = 'perfect seeing').
segments.paradiso-5-g5: Che here is a relative pronoun ('which') linking the preceding idea (perfetto veder) to the relative clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-5-g6: Come introduces a comparison/simile ('as' / 'just as'). Apprende is 3rd‑person singular present of apprendere in the sense 'it apprehends / grasps' (not merely 'learns'); it refers back to the perfect seeing mentioned above.
segments.paradiso-5-g7: Così = 'thus / so'. Nel bene appreso uses the past participle appreso ('grasped / perceived') modifying bene ('the good'); move is 3rd‑person singular present ('moves / sets in motion') and il piede ('the foot') is a metaphorical expression for a first step or movement toward that good. The phrase means: just as the act of apprehension moves the will (the 'foot') toward the good perceived.
segments.paradiso-5-g8: Io veggio is 'I see / I perceive' (veggio is an archaic poetic verb). Ben sì is an emphatic modifier ('quite / indeed'). Come introduces the clause 'how / that', già = 'already', resplende is 3rd‑person present 'shines', and ne l’intelletto tuo l’etterna luce places the subject (the eternal light) after the verb in poetic inversion: 'the eternal light shines in your intellect.'
segments.paradiso-5-g9: Che is the relative pronoun 'which' linking the shining of the eternal light to the following participial phrase; here it introduces the descriptive clause about that light.
segments.paradiso-5-g10: Vista is the past participle of vedere used absolutely ('seen' / 'when seen'). It modifies the eternal light (understood from the previous clause): 'which, when seen,...'.
segments.paradiso-5-g11: Sola e sempre qualifies amore accende: sola = 'alone/only', e = 'and', sempre = 'always'. Accende is 3rd‑person present of accendere here in the sense 'kindles' or 'sets on fire' (metaphorically: excites love).
segments.paradiso-5-g12: E s’altra cosa is an archaic contraction of e se altra cosa ('and if some other thing'). Vostro amor is 'your love' (vostro = your / yours), and seduce is 3rd‑person present of sedurre 'seduces / draws away' — the clause asks what happens if some other object draws your love away.
segments.paradiso-5-g13: Non è se non is a fixed idiom meaning 'it is nothing but / it is only'. Di quella alcun vestigio = 'some trace of that' (di quella = 'of that', alcun vestigio = 'some trace'). The overall meaning: if something else seduces your love, it is merely some faint trace of that same light.
segments.paradiso-5-g14: Mal is an adverb meaning 'badly' or 'ill', and conosciuto is the past participle 'known' (here 'ill‑understood' or 'poorly known'). Together they qualify vestigio: 'a trace poorly understood.'
segments.paradiso-5-g15: Che is the relative pronoun 'which' referring to the vestigio; quivi is an archaic locative adverb meaning 'there', and traluce is 3rd‑person present of traluccere/tralucere 'shine through / gleam through'.
segments.paradiso-5-g16: Tu vuo' saper = 'you want to know' (vuo' is an archaic contraction of vuoi). Se = 'whether / if' (introduces an indirect question). Con altro servigio = 'by/with another service' (servigio meaning an act of service or devotion).
segments.paradiso-5-g17: Per manco voto = 'through/for a lesser vow': per introduces the instrumental/reason sense ('by/through / for'), manco = 'less / lesser', and voto = 'vow'. The phrase modifies the means by which the service would be performed.
segments.paradiso-5-g18: Si può is the impersonal/passive construction meaning 'one can / it is possible'. Render tanto = 'render / make so much' (tanto = 'so much / such an extent'). Che introduces the result clause 'that...', and l’anima sicuri di letigio means 'the soul secure from litigation' (sicuri = 'secure' used predicatively; di letigio = 'from quarrel/litigation').
segments.paradiso-5-g19: Sì = 'thus' / 'so'. Cominciò is 3rd‑person singular past ('began'). Beatrice is the subject and questo canto is the direct object; word order is inverted for poetic effect: 'Thus Beatrice began this song.'
segments.paradiso-5-g20: E sì com' = 'and just as'. Uom is the archaic form of uomo ('man'). Che introduces a relative clause: 'who does not break / interrupt his speech' (suo parlar non spezza). Spezza is present indicative of spezzare ('break'), used idiomatically for interrupting speech. The clause characterizes uninterrupted discourse.
segments.paradiso-5-g21: Continüò is 3rd‑person singular past 'continued / proceeded'. Così = 'thus', and ’l processo santo = 'the holy process / discourse' (the article is contracted), indicating that the sacred argument continued without interruption.
segments.paradiso-5-g22: Lo maggior don = 'the greatest gift' (maggior modifies don). Che introduces a relative clause 'that God ... made in creating'. Per sua larghezza = 'by his largesse / generosity' (per = 'by / through', sua larghezza = 'his generosity'), and creando is a gerund ('in creating'). Fesse is an archaic past form meaning 'made' (poetic / old conjugation of fare) and governs creando.
segments.paradiso-5-g23: E a la sua bontate = 'and more conformed to his goodness': a = 'to', la sua bontate = 'his goodness'. Più conformato is a past participle phrase 'more conformed', agreeing with 'don' (the gift) described previously.
segments.paradiso-5-g24: E quel ch’e’ più apprezza = 'and that which he most esteems': quel = 'that', ch’ = 'which', e’ is the archaic clitic of 'he' (egli), più apprezza = 'most esteems / values most'. The clause further qualifies the gift.
segments.paradiso-5-g25: Fu is the past tense 'was'. De la volontà = 'of the will' (de = di, genitive), la libertate = 'the liberty / freedom'. The line states that the greatest gift was the freedom of the will.
segments.paradiso-5-g26: Di che = 'of which' (relative linking back to la libertate). Le creature intelligenti = 'the intelligent creatures' (le = the; creature = creatures; intelligenti = intelligent/perceptive). The phrase begins a relative clause explaining who received that freedom.
segments.paradiso-5-g27: E tutte e sole is an emphatic phrase: e = 'and', tutte = 'all', e sole = 'and only/alone' — it stresses that the intelligent creatures, all of them and alone, have this endowment (exclusive to them).
segments.paradiso-5-g28: Fuoro e son are two finite verbs (past 'were' and present 'are') used together to say 'were and are endowed.' Dotate is the past participle of dotare ('to endow'), agreeing with le creature: they were endowed and are still endowed with liberty of will.
segments.paradiso-5-g63: This is a prohibitive jussive phrasing: 'Ma non trasmuti ... alcun' uses the subjunctive form trasmuti (3rd singular) in a sense equivalent to a negative command ('let no one transfer'). carco is a noun/adjective meaning a load/burden placed on the shoulder; per suo arbitrio (literally 'by his own arbitrium') indicates the agent's private will or discretion. The clause as a whole: 'But let no one by his own will transfer a burden onto his shoulder.'
segments.paradiso-5-g64: sanza is an archaic form of senza ('without'). volta here means 'turn/assent/approval' (a legal or procedural 'turn' or allowance) rather than only 'time' or architectural 'vault'. The phrase lists things that must not be lacking: 'without the turn/assent and without the white key and the yellow one.' Chiave is the literal 'key' and the colors qualify those keys.
segments.paradiso-5-g65: Here credi is an exhortative/present form (you should think/consider) addressing the reader/listener; ogne permutanza means 'every exchange' or 'any change of position'. stolta is a predicate adjective ('foolish'). The line therefore instructs: 'and consider every exchange foolish.'
segments.paradiso-5-g66: This conditional clause begins with se ('if') and has la cosa dimessa (the thing put aside/dismissed) with dimessa as a past participle functioning adjectivally. in la sorpresa means 'in the surprise' or 'in the case of a sudden event/ambush' and non è raccolta is a present passive/compound sense ('is not gathered/collected'). come ’l quattro nel sei is a simile comparing how things are not gathered together — 'as the four in the six (are not gathered).' The whole: 'if the thing laid aside in the surprise is not gathered together like the four in the six.'
segments.paradiso-5-g67: Però is a discourse connector ('therefore'/'however'). qualunque cosa is an indefinite relative phrase ('whatever thing'/'anything'); tanto ... che marks a result clause ('so much ... that'). pesa is present indicative 'weighs'; per suo valor ('by/ because of its value') gives the measure; che tragga ogne bilancia is a result relative clause—tragga is present subjunctive/optative used to express the consequential effect ('that it should tip or draw every scale').
segments.paradiso-5-g68: This is an impersonal passive construction: sodisfar (archaic infinitive 'to satisfy/compensate') with non si può ('it is not possible') expresses inability — 'cannot be satisfied.' con altra spesa ('with another expense/outlay') specifies that no other payment/expenditure can make it right. The order is typical Dantean inversion for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-5-g69: Non prendan is a prohibitive jussive use of the subjunctive (3rd plural) meaning 'let them not take.' li mortali is the noun phrase subject with the archaic article li. il voto a ciancia means 'the vow as chatter/idle talk' — a warning not to treat vows lightly.
segments.paradiso-5-g70: siate is the present subjunctive/imperative 2nd-person-plural used here as an instruction: 'be (you all) faithful.' fedeli is a plural predicate adjective addressing the listeners.
segments.paradiso-5-g71: e a ciò ('and with respect to that' / 'as to this') frames the following admonition. far is the infinitive 'to do' and non bieci is an archaic adjective/verb-form meaning 'do not swerve/turn aside' (i.e. 'do not deviate from performing it'). The sense is: 'and do not stray from doing this.'
segments.paradiso-5-g72: come introduces a comparison ('as'); Ieptè is the biblical Jephthah. a la sua prima mancia means 'to/with his first offering/gift' — referring to Jephthah’s rash vow concerning his first offering.
segments.paradiso-5-g73: cui is a relative pronoun 'to whom'; più si convenia is an impersonal past notion meaning 'it would have been more fitting'; dicer (archaic infinitive for dire) introduces the quoted repentance 'Mal feci' ('I have done ill'). The clause says that it would have been better for him to confess 'I did evil.'
segments.paradiso-5-g74: che here links the comparative clause ('than'), functioning as 'than'/'that' connecting the previous remark to the following contrast.
segments.paradiso-5-g75: servando is a gerund/participle from servare ('keeping, preserving') and means 'by keeping/observing (the vow)'; it introduces the manner that would have produced a worse result.
segments.paradiso-5-g76: far peggio: far is the infinitive 'to do' and peggio is the comparative 'worse' — 'to do worse.' It completes the contrast: better to confess than, in keeping the vow, to do worse.
segments.paradiso-5-g77: Inverted order: 'e così stolto ritrovar puoi il gran duca de’ Greci'—ritrovar puoi (infinitive + poter in present second person) means 'you can find' and stolto is the predicative adjective applying to 'il gran duca de’ Greci' (the great leader/duke of the Greeks). The sense: 'and so you can find the great leader of the Greeks foolish.'
segments.paradiso-5-g78: onde here functions as 'whence' or 'whereupon/therefore' (a connective adverb explaining consequence). pianse is passato remoto 'she/wept' and Efigènia is the subject; il suo bel volto is the direct object in a poetic sense ('her beautiful face' as the thing she wept).
segments.paradiso-5-g79: This clause contains a causative structure: fé (fece) + infinitive pianger = 'caused to weep.' di sé ('about herself' / 'on her account') explains the cause; i folli e i savi ('the fools and the wise') are the people affected. The relative ch’udir parlar di così fatto cólto modifies 'i folli e i savi' — 'who heard talk of so accomplished/attained a deed.'
segments.paradiso-5-g80: Siate is the present subjunctive/imperative 2nd-person-plural used as an address: 'Be (you all)...' It functions as a direct exhortation to the addressees.
segments.paradiso-5-g81: Cristiani is the vocative noun addressing the audience: 'Christians.' The comma indicates direct address.
segments.paradiso-5-g82: a muovervi più gravi: a + infinitive muovervi ('to move yourselves' / 'in your actions') expresses the domain in which they should be serious; muovervi is reflexive with clitic vi 'yourselves', and più gravi is the comparative/adjective 'more serious/weighty.'
segments.paradiso-5-g83: non siate is the negative imperative ('do not be'); come penna ad ogne vento is a simile: 'like a feather in every wind' — i.e. easily blown about. The line warns against being fickle.
segments.paradiso-5-g84: e non crediate is another negative exhortation 'and do not believe.' The subordinate clause ch’ogne acqua vi lavi uses ch’ + subjunctive lavi (present subjunctive) to express what they should not imagine: that every water will wash/cleanse you (vi = you). The tone is cautionary: not every externals cleanse or absolve.
segments.paradiso-5-g95: Così is an adverb meaning 'thus' placed at the start for emphasis. The phrase Beatrice a me marks the subject (Beatrice) and the indirect object (to me). com' is a contraction of come = 'as', ïo is the emphatic subject pronoun 'io' (written with dieresis to show the vowel is pronounced), and scrivo is the present indicative 'I write' used parenthetically ('as I write'). The semicolon shows that this clause is a parenthetical remark about how Beatrice spoke.
segments.paradiso-5-g96: poi = 'then' (temporal). si rivolse is a pronominal verb (rivolversi) in passato remoto, third person singular, meaning 'she turned (herself)'; tutta disïante is an adverbial phrase meaning 'wholly longing' or 'completely desirous' (tutta modifies disïante). a quella parte = 'toward that part/side'; ove is a relative/adverb 'where'; ’l (elided il) mondo è più vivo = 'the world is more alive/liveliest' (più + vivo = comparative).
segments.paradiso-5-g97: Lo and l are definite articles placed before the abstract nouns tacere ('silence') and trasmutar sembiante ('the changing of countenance'); Dante treats these infinitives as nouns. puoser is an archaic past tense (passato remoto) meaning 'they put/placed' (modern posero) and agrees with the compound subject ('her silence and the changing of her expression'), so it means 'they caused' or 'put' silence. silenzio al mio cupido ingegno = 'silence upon my desirous/intense intellect' where cupido functions as 'desirous' or 'eager' modifying ingegno.
segments.paradiso-5-g98: che is a relative pronoun referring back to 'my eager intellect' (mio cupido ingegno). già = 'already'; nuove questioni = 'new questions'; aveva (avea) is the imperfect tense, indicating an ongoing or habitual past state ('had'); davante = 'before' or 'already beforehand' (i.e., questions already pending).
segments.paradiso-5-g99: e sì come introduces a simile: 'and just as'. saetta here functions as 'an arrow/bolt' in poetic usage. che nel segno percuote = 'that strikes the mark' (nel segno = into/on the target); pria che + subjunctive (pria che sia) is a temporal clause meaning 'before the bowstring is/becomes quiet' — sia is present subjunctive of essere because of the 'che' after a temporal adverb (pria che). percuote is present in the protasis of the simile ('strikes'), and queta modifies corda = 'quiet/still string' (the bowstring).
segments.paradiso-5-g100: così picks up the simile ('thus/so'), corremmo is passato remoto first-person plural of correre = 'we ran' or 'we rushed', and nel secondo regno is the destination 'into the second realm/kingdom' (nel = in + il). The whole clause says that, like that arrow, they swiftly moved into the second realm.
segments.paradiso-5-g101: Quivi = 'there' (locative). The word order is emphatic: la donna mia (my lady) as direct object precedes the verb vid' (an archaic short form of vidi = 'I saw'), followed by the subject pronoun io for emphasis. sì lieta = 'so/so very glad'.
segments.paradiso-5-g102: come = 'as' introduces the manner; nel lume di quel ciel = 'in the light of that heaven' (nel = in + il). si mise is the pronominal verb mettersi in passato remoto, third person singular, meaning 'she placed herself/positioned herself' (i.e., she moved into that light).
segments.paradiso-5-g103: che introduces a result clause: 'so that'. più lucente = 'more shining' or 'brighter'. se ne fé combines the reflexive se and the particle ne ('of it/from it') with fé (archaic past of fare = 'made/caused'), meaning 'the planet made itself more brilliant from it' → 'the planet became brighter because of her.' l' pianeta = 'the planet' (article elided).
segments.paradiso-5-g104: E se = 'and if' (a conditional introducer); la stella = 'the star'. si cambiò is reflexive passato remoto, third person singular of cambiare = 'changed itself' (i.e., 'changed'). e rise: rise is passato remoto of ridere in the sense 'smiled/laughed' (here 'smiled' or 'smiled upon'). Together the clause gives a conditional: 'And if the star changed and smiled...'.
segments.paradiso-5-g105: qual mi fec' io = rhetorical/exclamatory question 'what did I become/make of myself?' (fec' is Dante's archaic past of fare, i.e. feci/facesti forms). che pur da mia natura trasmutabile son per tutte guise = 'for/seeing that I am by my nature changeable in every fashion'—pur = 'indeed/still', da mia natura = 'by my nature', trasmutabile = 'changeable', son = present 'I am', per tutte guise = 'in all guises/ways'.
segments.paradiso-5-g123: Così = 'thus' (adverb) introduces the manner. 'da un di quelli spirti pii' is an agent phrase introduced by da = 'by' (agent of a passive construction). 'detto mi fu' is a perfect passive construction with the past participle detto and the auxiliary fu (archaic past of essere), plus the clitic mi = 'to me' (indirect object); literal order is 'said to me was'.
segments.paradiso-5-g124: e = 'and' linking the previous clause to the following speaker-mark; da = 'by' again indicating agency; Beatrice is the proper name of the speaker (vocative/agent noted separately).
segments.paradiso-5-g125: Dì here is the vocative/imperative form of dire = 'say' (poetically written); it addresses the addressee to speak. The provided lemma senses list is ambiguous, so the imperative reading is explained rather than matched to the listed senses.
segments.paradiso-5-g126: dì (imperative of dire) repeated for emphasis = 'say'; sicuramente is an adverb modifying the verb, meaning 'certainly' or 'for sure'. The comma marks the pause before the following clause.
segments.paradiso-5-g127: e = 'and' linking the command to the following imperative credi (2nd sing. imperative of credere = 'believe'); come a dii = a short comparative/idiomatic phrase 'as to gods' / 'as one would (believe) to gods' (come = 'as', a = 'to', dii = 'gods').
segments.paradiso-5-g128: Io = 'I' subject. veggio is a poetical/older verb meaning 'I see' / 'I behold' (here functioning like vedere); ben sì = 'very well' / 'indeed'. come introduces the subordinate clause 'how/as' and tu t’annidi = tu + clitic t' + annidi (2nd sing. present indicative of annidare) = 'you nestle/settle yourself'; nel proprio lume = 'in your own light' (preposition + possessive + noun).
segments.paradiso-5-g129: e che = 'and that' introduces a second subordinate clause; de li occhi = 'from the eyes' (de = archaic di); il traggi = verb traggi (2nd sing. present) with object il (the light referred back) = 'you draw it'. Note the object il is placed before the verb, a common clitic/old ordering.
segments.paradiso-5-g130: perch’ = perché 'because'; e’ (here) = clitic or archaic pronoun referring to the light ('it'); corusca = verb (coruscare) 'it flashes/sparkles' (poetic form); sì come tu ridi = 'just as you laugh' (sì come = 'just as' / 'as'). Some forms are archaic/poetic spellings.
segments.paradiso-5-g131: ma non so = 'but I do not know' (negation with non before verb sapere). chi tu se’ = 'who you are' (se' = archaic/clitic form of sei/are). The clause is an indirect question introduced by chi.
segments.paradiso-5-g132: né = 'nor' continuing the negation; perché = 'why'; aggi is an archaic/uncertain verb form (lemma given as aggire) here meaning something like 'you go about' or 'you move' — its precise action is poetic and context-determined (sense not clear in the supplied list).
segments.paradiso-5-g133: anima degna = a vocative or direct address, 'worthy soul' (anima = 'soul/person', degna = 'worthy'), set off by commas and addressing the apparition.
segments.paradiso-5-g134: il grado de la spera = 'the degree/rank of the sphere' (grado = rank/degree; de la = of the); che si vela = relative clause 'which veils itself' (si reflexive = 'itself'); a’ mortai = 'to mortals' (mortai is an archaic/poetic form for mortali — 'mortals'); con altrui raggi = 'with others' rays' (altrui = 'of others', raggi = 'rays').
segments.paradiso-5-g135: Questo = 'this' (demonstrative). diss' = contracted poetic past tense of dire (disse) = 'I said'; io = subject 'I' (emphatic). diritto = 'directly/straight' (adverbial), a la lumera = 'to the luminary/light' (indirect object introduced by a). che pria m’avea parlato = relative clause 'that before had spoken to me' (pria = 'before', m' = clitic 'to me', avea = imperfect auxiliary, parlato = past participle).
segments.paradiso-5-g136: ond’ = 'whence/whereupon' (consequence indicator). ella = 'she/it' (the light). fessi is an archaic/poetic form meaning 'fece' (made) or 'si fece' = 'made herself' (the lexical senses provided do not list 'make', so the form is irregular here). lucente più assai di quel ch’ell’ era = 'brighter much than she had been' (comparative structure: lucente = 'bright', più assai = 'much more', di quel che ella era = 'than she was').
segments.paradiso-5-g137: Sì come = 'just as' (simile). il sol = 'the sun' (here an archaic/poetic spelling; word-order is normal). che si cela = relative clause 'that hides itself' (si = reflexive). elli stessi = emphatic reflexive 'itself' (poetic duplication), per troppa luce = 'because of/for too much light' (per + noun indicating cause).
segments.paradiso-5-g138: come = 'as/just as' continuing the comparison; ’l caldo ha róse = 'the heat has ...' (ha = present of avere), róse is a poetic/archaic verb-form here (sense unclear in supplied list) affecting the following noun; le temperanze d’i vapori spessi = 'the temperings/moderations of the thick vapors' (temperanze = 'moderating effects' or 'temperances', d’i = of the, spessi = 'thick/dense').
segments.paradiso-5-g139: per più letizia = 'through/for more joy' (per + comparative phrase); sì mi si nascose = reflexive/impersonal structure with two clitics (mi = 'to me', si = reflexive) and past nascose (hid itself) = 'it hid itself from me'; dentro al suo raggio = 'within its ray'; la figura santa = 'the holy figure' (subject of nascose).
segments.paradiso-5-g140: e così = 'and thus/so'; chiusa chiusa = reduplicated participle 'closed, closed' (poetic emphasis; first chiusa adjectival, second verbal/iterative), mi rispuose = 'she answered me' (past rispose/rispuose, mi = 'to me'); nel modo che ’l seguente canto canta = 'in the way that the following canto sings' (relative clause identifying the manner).
segments.paradiso-5-g106: This is a simile introduced by Come ('as'). ’n is a contracted/informal form of in (here: 'in a peschiera' = 'in a fishpond'). peschiera is the feminine noun 'fishpond'; ch’ is the relative che (that/which) introducing the clause ‘è tranquilla e pura’ where è is present indicative 'is' and the adjectives tranquilla e pura agree with peschiera. traggonsi is a pronominal verb (third plural present + enclitic -si) meaning 'are drawn' or 'draw themselves' (a passive/reflective construction) with i pesci as subject. a ciò che vien di fori = 'to that which comes from outside' (ciò che = 'that which'; vien = 'comes'; di fori is an archaic/poetic locution meaning 'from outside'). Per modo che introduces the result clause 'in such a way that' and lo is the neuter pronoun 'it'; stimin is an archaic third-person plural meaning 'they esteem' (think/regard), lor = their, pastura = 'pasture/food' (i.e. 'they regard it as their food').
segments.paradiso-5-g107: sì (thus/so) links the simile to what follows. vid' io is the poet's elided past tense vidi ('I saw'); ben is an intensifier 'indeed/truly'. più di mille splendori = 'more than a thousand splendors' (di here = 'than'). trarsi is the infinitive/pronominal form meaning 'to move oneself' or 'to draw oneself'; it is the action seen by the narrator. ver' is an archaic contraction of verso ('toward') and noi = 'us', so trarsi ver' noi = 'move toward us'.
segments.paradiso-5-g108: e in ciascun s'udia is an impersonal/passive construction: s' (si) + imperfect udia yields 'and in each one was heard' or 'in each one one could hear'. ciascun = 'each one' (each splendour) and s'udia expresses that a phrase or sound was heard coming from each.
segments.paradiso-5-g109: Quoted utterance. Ecco is the demonstrative/exclamatory 'behold/here is'; chi is the relative 'who' (introducing the subordinate clause); crescerà is future third-person singular 'will grow/increase'; li is the old plural article 'the' used before nostri amori (our loves), which is the direct object. The line announces 'Behold who will increase our loves.'
segments.paradiso-5-g110: E sì come introduces a comparison continuing the previous image: 'And just as'. ciascuno 'each one' (each splendour) is the subject; a noi venìa uses the imperfect venìa ('was coming/came') to indicate that each repeatedly approached us – imperfect habitual/past action.
segments.paradiso-5-g111: vedeasi is an impersonal/passive form (si + vedea/vede) meaning 'could be seen' or 'was seen' — Dante often uses this archaic form to report a vision. l'ombra piena di letizia = 'the shade/shadow full of joy'. nel folgór chiaro = 'in the clear/bright flash' (folgór = sudden radiance/lightning). che di lei uscia is a relative clause 'which issued from her'; di lei = 'from her'. uscia here is an archaic verbal form (note that the wordlist gave noun senses, but context shows it is the verb 'uscire' in an archaic form meaning 'issued/forth came').
segments.paradiso-5-g112: Pensa is the singular imperative 'Think' addressed to the reader; it begins an apostrophe asking the reader to consider a hypothetical.
segments.paradiso-5-g113: lettor is the vocative 'reader' (an archaic/truncated form of lettore) directly addressing the person reading the poem.
segments.paradiso-5-g114: se introduces a conditional ('if'); quel che qui s'inizia = 'that which is begun here' (s'inizia is the pronominal passive/present 'is begun'). non procedesse is an imperfect subjunctive or contrary-to-fact conditional 'did not proceed/advance' (Dante often uses this tense to set up hypothetical situations).
segments.paradiso-5-g115: come tu avresti di più savere angosciosa carizia = 'how you would have more knowledge of anguished tenderness.' avresti is the conditional of avere 'you would have' (here used with the noun phrase di più savere = 'more knowledge'); angosciosa carizia is a paradoxical phrase meaning 'painful/anguished affection/tenderness.'
segments.paradiso-5-g116: e per te vederai = 'and for your sake you will see' (vederai is future 'you will see/you will perceive'). come da questi m'era in disio d'udir lor condizioni = 'how from these I was in desire to hear their conditions' — da questi = 'from these (spirits)'; m'era in disio d'udir = 'I was in desire (i.e. eager) to hear'; lor condizioni = 'their conditions/circumstances/stories.' m'era uses the imperfect era to describe a continuing mental state in the past.
segments.paradiso-5-g117: sì come a li occhi mi fur manifesti = 'even as they were made manifest to my eyes.' fur is an archaic past form (they were) and manifesti is plural adjective 'manifest/evident' agreeing with the things made manifest; a li occhi mi = 'to my eyes' (a li occhi = 'to the eyes', mi = 'to me' as dative-of-personal-perception).
segments.paradiso-5-g118: O bene nato is a vocative address 'O well-/noble-born one'. a cui = 'to whom' introduces the relative clause; veder li troni del trïunfo etternal concede grazia = 'to whom seeing the thrones of the eternal triumph grants grace' (veder as infinitive; concede is present indicative 'grants'; grazia = favor/grace). prima che la milizia s'abbandoni = 'before the host/army withdraws/abandons itself' — milizia here means the heavenly host (i.e., angels) and s'abbandoni is an archaic subjunctive/reflective form 'withdraws' or 'abandons itself', used after prima che ('before').
segments.paradiso-5-g119: del lume che per tutto il ciel si spazia = 'of the light which ranges through the whole heaven' (che = relative 'which'; per tutto il ciel = 'throughout the whole heaven/celestial sphere'; si spazia = pronominal verb 'ranges/extends itself'). noi semo accesi: semo is the archaic form of siamo ('we are') and accesi 'kindled/illuminated' – 'we are kindled by that light.'
segments.paradiso-5-g120: e però = 'and therefore' (conjunctive adverb introducing the consequence/result from the preceding statement).
segments.paradiso-5-g121: se disii di noi chiarirti — se introduces a condition 'if'; disii here is an archaic/dialectal form meaning 'you desire' or 'you wish' (the list entry 'I said' is not the relevant sense here); di noi chiarirti = 'to have us make clear to you' (chiarirti = infinitive with enclitic ti 'to clarify for you'). The whole phrase means 'if you wish us to clarify things about ourselves to you.'
segments.paradiso-5-g122: a tuo piacer ti sazia: a tuo piacer = 'to your pleasure/as you wish'; ti sazia literally 'satisfies you' (sazia is third-person present of saziare 'to satiate' and here functions as the promise 'it will satisfy you' or an exhortation 'satisfy yourself'). The construction offers the reader permission to be satisfied according to his own pleasure.

sentences.paradiso-5-s17: You have the New and the Old Testament, and the shepherd of the Church who guides you; let this suffice for your salvation.
sentences.paradiso-5-s18: If evil greed cries out to you for something else, be men and not frantic sheep, so that the Jew among you may not laugh at you!
sentences.paradiso-5-s19: Do not act like a lamb that leaves its mother's milk, and simple and lustful, it fights with itself at its own pleasure!
sentences.paradiso-5-s5: Now it will seem to you, if you argue from here, the lofty worth of the vow, whether it has been so made that God consents when you consent; for, in establishing the pact between God and man, this treasure is made a victim of the sort I speak of, and is made so by His act.
sentences.paradiso-5-s6: So then, what can be returned as restitution?
sentences.paradiso-5-s7: If you think to make proper use of what I have offered you, you mean to turn an ill-natured person into a good work (i.e. to get a good result from a bad source).
sentences.paradiso-5-s8: You are now certain about the main point; but because the Holy Church grants a dispensation in this matter — which seems contrary to the truth I have revealed to you — it is still fitting to sit a little at table, for the harsh food you have taken still requires aid from your provisions.
sentences.paradiso-5-s9: Open your mind to what I reveal to you, and fix it there within.
sentences.paradiso-5-s10: Two things are fitting to the essence of this sacrifice: one is that which is done; the other is the propriety.
sentences.paradiso-5-s11: This latter is never erased unless it is observed; and above it one speaks so precisely: therefore the Jews were compelled to offer it, even though some offering was so changed, as to be known of them.
sentences.paradiso-5-s12: The other, which by matter has been made plain to you, can well be such that it is not invalidated if it is transformed by another matter.
sentences.paradiso-5-s1: If I set you aflame in the warmth of a love that lies beyond the manner seen on earth, so that I overcome the force of your countenance, do not be astonished, for this proceeds from perfect seeing, which, as it apprehends, thus in the good perceived sets the foot in motion.
sentences.paradiso-5-s2: I clearly see how the eternal light already shines in your intellect, which, when seen, alone and always kindles love; and if some other thing seduces your love, it is nothing but some trace of that, ill‑understood, which gleams there.
sentences.paradiso-5-s3: You want to know whether by some other service, by a lesser vow, it is possible to do so much that the soul is safe from litigation.
sentences.paradiso-5-s4: Thus Beatrice began this canto; and, just as a man who does not interrupt his speech, the holy discourse continued in this way: 'The greatest gift that God, by his generosity, made in creating, and which is more conformed to his goodness and which he most esteems, was the freedom of the will; of which the intelligent creatures, all and alone, were and are endowed.'
sentences.paradiso-5-s13: But let no one shift a burden onto his shoulder by his own will, without the assent and without the white key and the yellow one; and consider every exchange foolish, if the thing laid aside in the surprise is not gathered together like the four into the six.
sentences.paradiso-5-s14: Therefore anything that weighs so much in its own worth that it draws every scale cannot be satisfied by another expenditure.
sentences.paradiso-5-s15: Let mortals not make vows as mere chatter; be faithful, and do not swerve from doing this, as Jephthah with his first gift; to whom it would have been more fitting to say 'I have done ill' than, by keeping it, to do worse; and thus you may find the great leader of the Greeks foolish, whence Iphigenia wept her fair face, and caused both fools and the wise to weep who heard talk of so consummate an action.
sentences.paradiso-5-s16: Be, Christians, more grave in your actions: do not be like a feather in every wind, and do not believe that every water will wash you.
sentences.paradiso-5-s21: Thus Beatrice, as I write, spoke to me; then she turned, wholly longing, toward that side where the world is liveliest.
sentences.paradiso-5-s22: Her silence and the changing of her expression imposed silence on my eager intellect, which already had new questions before; and just as an arrow strikes the mark before the bowstring is still, so we rushed into the second realm.
sentences.paradiso-5-s23: There I saw my lady so joyful, as she set herself in the light of that heaven, that the planet became more brilliant from it.
sentences.paradiso-5-s24: And if the star changed and smiled, what became of me—since by my nature I am changeable in every guise!
sentences.paradiso-5-s29: Thus was it said to me by one of those pious spirits; and by Beatrice: "Say — say it surely, and believe as (you would) to gods."
sentences.paradiso-5-s30: I see well how you nestle in your own light, and that you draw it from your eyes, for it flashes as you laugh; but I do not know who you are, nor why, worthy soul, you (so) move, the rank of the sphere that veils itself from mortals with others' rays.
sentences.paradiso-5-s31: I said this directly to the light that had spoken to me before; whereupon she made herself far more bright than she had been.
sentences.paradiso-5-s32: Just as the sun conceals itself for too much light, as the heat has tempered the dense vapors, so by greater joy the holy figure hid herself within its ray from me; and thus, closed, closed, she answered me in the way that the following canto sings.
sentences.paradiso-5-s25: As in a fishpond that is calm and clear the fish are drawn to whatever comes from outside, in such a way that they take it for their food; so I truly saw more than a thousand splendors move toward us, and in each one was heard: "Behold who will increase our loves."
sentences.paradiso-5-s26: And just as each one came to us, the shade full of joy could be seen in the bright flash that issued from her.
sentences.paradiso-5-s27: Think, reader, if what is begun here did not go on, how much more knowledge of anguished tenderness you would have; and for your sake you will see how from these I was in desire to hear their conditions, even as they were made manifest to my eyes.
sentences.paradiso-5-s28: "O well-born one, to whom seeing the thrones of the eternal triumph grants grace before the host withdraws: we are kindled by the light that ranges throughout the whole heaven; and therefore, if you wish to have us clarify matters about ourselves, satisfy yourself as pleases you."
